description_long: This object is a stylized flower with broad, flat petals radiating outward from a central point. The petals are irregularly shaped, with jagged edges and a slightly undulating surface, giving the flower a rugged, organic appearance. The coloration is predominantly a muted red with hints of greenish-brown, suggesting a weathered or natural look. The petals are arranged in a loose, radial pattern, with some overlapping and others spaced apart, creating a sense of depth and dimensionality. There is no visible stem or leaves attached to the flower, and the structure appears low to the ground, as if it is a ground-covering or basal flower. The surface texture appears matte and slightly rough, consistent with natural plant material.
description: A rugged, red and greenish-brown flower with broad, jagged petals radiating outward in a loose, radial pattern. The flower sits low to the ground with no visible stem or leaves.
description_view_0: This view shows the flower from above, highlighting the broad, flat petals and their radial arrangement around the center.
description_view_1: From this angle, the overlapping and uneven heights of the petals are more apparent, emphasizing the flower's organic, irregular form.
description_view_2: This view accentuates the spread of the petals and the open, star-like shape of the flower, with the central area clearly visible.
description_view_3: Here, the jagged edges and varying angles of the petals are most prominent, giving the flower a dynamic, layered appearance.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a loose, radial configuration, radiating outward from a central point with some overlapping and irregular spacing. The arrangement lacks strict symmetry but maintains a generally circular outline, typical of many basal flowers.
